question_text: A student builds two electromagnets: one with a 9V battery and one with a 3V battery, both with the same wire coil. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The 3V electromagnet will be stronger because less heat is wasted

options.label: B
options.text: The 9V electromagnet will be stronger because higher voltage creates more current

options.label: C
options.text: Both electromagnets will be equally strong because they use the same coil

options.label: D
options.text: The 3V electromagnet will be stronger because it uses less energy

correct_answer: The 9V electromagnet will be stronger because higher voltage creates more current
explanation: In a circuit with the same resistance (same wire coil), higher voltage causes more current to flow through the coil. Since the strength of an electromagnet depends on the current flowing through it, the 9V battery produces a stronger magnetic field than the 3V battery.
